admission_accounting.granularity_note: committed items restore 25 (ration) / 50 (energy drink) nominal; under effective accounting a deliberate top-up purchase below full restore value is admissible (e.g. a ration at energy 50 gains 10), so a greedy adversary can exhaust any C. Its coin cost is the full item price — cost is tracked per item bought, never per effective unit. Engine-truth risk registered at design time.
admission_accounting.reason: the registered drink-mix invariance gate ('cap accounted in energy units — mix moves purchase-action count only') holds only under effective-energy accounting: nominal accounting makes waste count against the cap and mixes inequivalent. Intake-time decision, registered before the runner.

windows.fixed-hour: admit iff (sum of admitted effective energy at times in [floor(t/3600)*3600, t]) + gain <= C
windows.sliding-3600s: admit iff (sum of admitted effective energy at times in (t-3600, t]) + gain <= C

b0_parent_anchors.INT_1_note: registered assumption inherited from V042 (UI/latency floor, not an engine constant). Cap-family robustness stated: once C binds, sustained digs/h = 360 + C regardless of the interaction floor.

ceiling_row_755_56.derivation: diamond tool x1.5 -> E[amount] = (round(1.5)+round(3.0))/2 = 5/2; ore_weights_for_depth(4) {stone .5, bronze .5, iron 4, silver 3.5, gold 3, diamond 2.5} -> E[value] = 151/28; product 755/56 — the V043 hand-pin, reproduced through the engine at B0

fishing_reporting_max_coins_per_energy.note: V043 committed grid max (deep_water/master). 40797/4000 = 10.19925 < 755/56 = 13.482... by arithmetic, so every fishing row is less binding than the ceiling row; fishing rides reporting-only.

b0_parent_anchors.note: Every anchor re-derived through the byte-copied engine (engine/games/mining/core/*, sha256 MANIFEST re-verified before import; same subtree as the parent, pin 57f69be3). ANY mismatch = validity failure -> honest NULL; no lever cell counts.

np1_anchors.time_model: the parent's own digs_time: first 60 digs @ INT-1 (2 s), rest @ 10 s/dig => seconds = 10*D - 480 for D > 60 (identity checked at B0)

PACE.rule: engine-measured boosted NP-1 ladder seconds >= 0.8 x the parent's committed throttled anchors, for BOTH Forge I (4476179765/1146072 s) and Forge II (17222779765/1146072 s)

REFILL.rule: the cap admits >= one full-bar emergency refill per window (0 -> MAX_ENERGY = 60 effective within a single window, i.e. C >= 60) AND that refill at the cell's prices costs <= 2880/7 coins (committed items: drink+ration = 60 coins). REPRICE cells score refill cost as 60e (the registered divisible-restore arithmetic that produced the 48/7 bound); the committed-granularity cost (75e nominal) ships as a reporting column.

SEAL.rule: engine-measured BOOST-FARMER mean net coins/h over the 8 h horizon <= 5/4 x the SAME cell-horizon's measured THROTTLED control net coins/h, at EVERY committed value row {P0, P1, P2, P3, 755/56}. Denominator = the measured C=0 control (identical horizon + initial full bar, so the shared bar burst cancels; the parent's 360E/h sustained rate is a B0 anchor, not the denominator). Fishing rows reporting-only (max 40797/4000 < 755/56 by arithmetic).

bands.note: registered judgment lines; full tables ship so a re-drawn line re-reads, never re-runs (the parent's own rule).

controls.C0: cap 0 admits nothing == throttled control (identity gate)
controls.Cinf: no cap at committed e = 4/5 == the parent's bypass anchor (identity gate)

cells.note: 18 registered cells. Enumeration (intake-time decision, disclosed): 1 B0-regression cell (throttled control, no cap layer) + C=0 and C=inf identity cells under EACH semantics (4) + 8 decision cells (C x semantics) + 4 REPRICE cells (C=inf, e-grid) + 1 HYBRID = 18. Only the 8 decision cells can decide; all others are gates or reporting.

closed_form_predictions_pre_run.admitted_per_decision_cell: 8*C over the 8 h horizon in BOTH semantics (admissions at ~0, 3600, ..., 25200; sliding has no extra fencepost inside 28800 s)
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.control_digs_note: 2880 regen digs + full-bar burst ~75 (bar 60 amplified by regen during the burst, 60/(1 - 2/10)) - terminal rounding; engine-exact value measured, band pre-registered
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.farmer_digs_identity: farmer digs == control digs + admitted effective energy (regen is cap-independent below MAX_ENERGY; identity gate, exact)
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.ladder_fluid_predictor: piecewise supply/demand: digs(t) = min(t/2, 60 + t/10 + A(t)), A(t) = admitted by t (C per window, front-loaded). Engine-vs-fluid agreement tolerance +-120 s (integer regen remainders + purchase granularity). FRONT-LOAD RISK registered at design time: a greedy ladder runner consumes the whole first-window cap immediately, so short goals (Forge I ~ 439 digs) see boost density far above the sustained rate — the sustained-rate PACE ratios (e.g. 0.833 at C=75 Forge I) are expected to be OPTIMISTIC; the measured gap IS the engine premium on the ladder leg.

mc_tolerance.se_arithmetic: per-dig coin sd/mean <= ~0.75 at every row (finite support {1..3 amounts} x {1..12 values}); cell MC at M=400 x >=3000 digs -> SE/mean <= 0.75/sqrt(1.2e6) ~ 0.0007; row MC at N=200000 -> ~0.0017. A 0.01 relative gate is >= 5.9 sigma per comparison, familywise-safe over the 13 MC gate points (expected false-failure probability < 1e-7).

closed_form_predictions_pre_run.note: registered before the runner exists; the run must confirm or the discrepancy is reported as the engine premium

repice_exclusion_pair.statement: 11325/896 > 48/7 -> no price-only cell passes both bands; registered exclusion (the P033 rho=1.0 precedent), REPRICE rides reporting-only

closed_form_predictions_pre_run.rho_closed_form: rho(row, C) = 1 + C*(E - 4/5)/(360*E) (sustained-rate form, the proposal's drafting table)
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.seal_line: 5/4 -> closed form leaves {60, 75} live, kills {100, 125} at the ceiling row (C=100 passes P3 at 4787/3838 ~ 1.2473 — knife-edge, disclosed)

decision_rule.APPROVE: >= 2 decision cells within ONE window semantics pass all three bands, stability-reproduced (the P031 two-consecutive-cells rule; a band, not a knife-edge point) -> recommend the cheapest-diff passing cell (registered expectation: sliding C=75 by closed form, margin 0.054 at the binding row — must survive the engine premium)
decision_rule.NULL: anything else (semantics-conditional or single-cell passes) -> the conditional rule is the citable finding, with the measured engine premium as the number; name the cheapest LIVE probe: shop telemetry (booster purchases/player/h + purchase-time energy level)
decision_rule.REJECT: no decision cell passes SEAL+REFILL+PACE, stability-reproduced -> 'no committed-shape cap seals the sink — redesign, not retune'; rescue family named (diminishing restore / boost-fatigue, its own future sim); the V042/V043/V045 ledger carries the measured boosted-column caveat meanwhile
decision_rule.intent_boundary: this verdict PRICES the menu, never rules intended-vs-not — V042's 'possibly intended per the shop's own comment' restated verbatim; the seat's/owner's call with the table in hand
decision_rule.order: REJECT checked FIRST, then APPROVE, else NULL (pre-registered; the fragile direction is APPROVE)

gates_run_invalid_on_failure: MANIFEST sha256 re-verified on all 13 engine files before import
B0: every parent anchor above re-derived exactly through the engine BEFORE any lever cell runs
C=0 cells (both semantics) byte-identical to the THROTTLED control timeline
C=inf @ e=4/5 cells: 1800 digs/h identity, purchased = digs - control, V042-accounting bypass ratio == 37535/8427 and regen-credited == 38455/8427 (exact Fractions in their registered accountings)
farmer dig identity: digs == control + admitted (every decision cell, both semantics)
independent sliding-window re-audit: max admitted effective energy over ALL trailing-3600s windows <= C on every sliding cell (and reported for fixed cells; BOUNDARY-GAMER expected 2C there)
drink-mix invariance: {drink-first, ration-only, alternating} mixes at (sliding, C=75) produce identical digs, admitted energy and coin spend; only purchase-action counts differ
affordability: cumulative coin spend <= cumulative earned at the evaluated row for every (cell, row) timeline (exact, post-hoc)
Arm-S ladder seconds within +-120 s of the Arm-A fluid predictor per (decision cell, forge)
twin independently-written decision evaluators (float-based and Fraction-based) agree on every band flag and the ruling, on main AND stability legs
MC legs within the pre-checked tolerances above
stability leg (seed 20261282, M=200) reproduces the ruling
stdout + results.json byte-identical across two full process runs (external diff)
CPython minor pinned: cpython-3.11 asserted at runtime

liveness_pre_registered.APPROVE: sliding {60, 75} clear every sustained-rate closed form with >= 0.054 margin at the binding row — reachable iff the measured engine premium (burst front-load, granularity, boundary gaming) does not eat that margin
liveness_pre_registered.NULL: expected candidate axes: the semantics fork (fixed-hour gaming premium) AND the ladder front-load premium registered above — a single-cell or semantics-conditional pass set lands NULL by the two-cells rule
liveness_pre_registered.REJECT: reachable iff the measured premium exceeds the margin on BOTH semantics at every C

out_of_scope: intent (priced, not ruled — the seat's/owner's call)
the amount-inert pickaxe flag (V042's OTHER row — named runner-up with its own future sim)
cross-session cap persistence (host territory, the V044 per-session precedent)
diminishing-restore / boost-fatigue mechanism changes (the REJECT-case rescue family, its own sim)

policies.BOOST-FARMER: greedy registered adversary: digs at INT-1; when energy = 0 buys the largest committed item admissible under the cell's window (drink 50 first, ration 25, then a top-up ration if the residual cap and profitability allow), only while an item's coin cost is < its effective energy gain x the evaluated row's E[coins/dig] (strict) and the remaining horizon can dig the gain; otherwise waits for the earlier of regen or window-free
policies.BOUNDARY-GAMER: fixed-hour-only reporting variant: defers each fixed window's purchases to the window's last admissible instant, then buys the next window's immediately — measures the worst trailing-3600s admitted energy (expected 2C) and the peak-window rate premium; cannot flip the decision (means are unchanged or worse)
policies.HONEST-CASUAL: plays [h*3600, h*3600+1800) each hour h = 0..7; digs at INT-1; at most ONE full-bar refill per hour (drink+ration bought at energy 0, if admissible); convenience-preservation reporting row
policies.NP1-LADDER: boosted ladder run per decision cell: BOOST-FARMER purchase logic, unbounded horizon, stops at the parent's committed NP-1 dig anchors (ceil applied to the fluid fractions). Booster coin spend's extra dig need is IGNORED — conservative against APPROVE (fewer digs = faster boosted ladder = harsher PACE); the cost-inflated closed-form variant ships as a reporting column.
policies.THROTTLED: digs at INT-1 whenever settled energy >= 1, waits for regen otherwise; never buys

preregistration.proposal: idea-engine control/outbox.md PROPOSAL 035 - 2026-07-13T12:12:40Z - status sim-ready; idea file ideas/superbot-games/mining-booster-bypass-throttle-seal-2026-07-13.md (idea-engine PR #308); claim: idea-engine PR #310 (control/claims, VERDICT 046 reserved)
preregistration.statement: This fixture file is the PRE-REGISTRATION for VERDICT 046. It was authored BEFORE the runner (booster_bypass_sim.py) was written or executed; the results reference it. Commit order in this branch shows engine+fixtures before the runner and results. Every parent anchor below was MACHINE-READ from the committed sims/verdict-042-mining-economy/{results,fixtures}.json and sims/verdict-043-fishing-economy/results.json at the branch base (origin/main c2c90f64c195a7bbda1747a490826db6387f996c; the proposal pinned the same values at ledger pin afe18f3) by a build-time extractor, never transcribed by hand; the derived constants were re-derived from those reads and asserted against the proposal's drafting arithmetic.

seeds.policy: allocated strictly above the fleet high-water 20261280 (V043's robustness leg, re-read at the ledger); new high-water 20261284

value_rows.note: coin outcomes never feed back into the timeline (policies condition only on profitability e < E_row, a per-(cell,row) constant), and the engine's energy/admission dynamics contain ZERO RNG (energy.py is deterministic; verified at B0). Each (cell, row) timeline is therefore deterministic; decision numbers are exact Fractions on its integer dig/purchase counts (the parent's zero-decision-bearing-RNG discipline). The seeded MC legs validate the per-dig coin means through the engine's own roll path (self-checks, cannot flip).
